Title: Overexpression of OsACL5 triggers environmentallydependent leaf rolling and reduces grain size in rice

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: shoots and leaves of 2-week-old seedlings; leaves under high temperature/high light/low humidity
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression markedly increases thermospermine, decreases spermidine and spermine, reduces ethylene evolution, causes environmentally dependent adaxial leaf rolling, smaller metaxylem vessels, shorter plants, smaller grains, lower 1000-grain weight and reduced yield; knockout increases grain length, 1000-grain weight and yield
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in all tested tissues, especially roots, stems and leaves; strong expression in vascular tissues, roots, embryos, seeds during grouting, floral meristem and mature stem vascular bundles
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed broadly in rice tissues, especially vasculature-associated tissues
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: maize ubiquitin promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; native OsACL5 promoter for GUS reporter; CaMV 35S promoter for subcellular localization construct
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High temperature, high light intensity and low humidity enhance leaf rolling and increase T-Spm in OE leaves; midday vs morning conditions used for RNA-seq
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; constitutive overexpression; in vitro enzyme assay; HPLC metabolite profiling; GC ethylene measurement; qRT-PCR; promoter-GUS; subcellular localization; RNA-seq; haplotype analysis
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina NovaSeq6000), accession NA; haplotype analysis using RiceVarMap v2.0
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsACL5 is the focal gene controlling thermospermine homeostasis in rice. 2. Overexpression elevates T-Spm more than ninefold and shifts polyamine/ethylene balance, while knockout improves grain length, 1000-grain weight and plot yield. 3. OsACL5 overexpression causes temperature/light/humidity-dependent leaf rolling associated with reduced metaxylem size and lower leaf water potential. 4. Natural variation analysis suggests a conserved role of OsACL5 in regulating T-Spm during rice domestication.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ACAULIS5/thermospermine synthase family; polyamine biosynthesis enzyme
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyamines support plant growth, vascular development and adaptation to environmental changes/stress
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although OsACL5 overexpression strongly increased T-Spm, osacl5 knockout did not significantly reduce T-Spm, suggesting compensation or dynamic back-conversion/homeostasis. Study concludes OsACL5 is valuable for yield-oriented breeding by reducing its activity rather than overexpressing it.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: OsACL5 is a promising target for rice yield improvement; knockout or reduced expression may increase grain size and yield, whereas overexpression is detrimental due to leaf rolling and yield penalties.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes shorter plants, thinner culms, smaller panicles, smaller grains, lower yield and environmentally sensitive leaf rolling.
